title: Laure Ferrari, the Frenchwoman whispering in Nigel Farage's ear
subhed: Little-known in France, Ferrari works as an adviser to her partner Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, currently leading the polls in the UK.

title: Russia stands by Mali's military junta at all costs

Trump says cutting US troop numbers in Germany 'further than 5,000'
subhed: The Pentagon announced the 5,000-troop reduction on Friday, but the US president told reporters Saturday 'we're going to cut way down, and we're cutting a lot further than 5,000.'

title: Gulf monarchies divided amid threats of new US strikes on Iran
subhed: During a visit to the Gulf countries, French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot spoke to the divided allies, who seek to achieve greater autonomy over their own defense while avoiding upsetting Washington.

title: Dries Van Noten's Venice palazzo showcases artworks and crafts from around the world
subhed: After a career in fashion, Dries Van Noten has opened a foundation in Venice's Palazzo Pisani Moretta with partner Patrick Vangheluwe to showcase artists and artisans they admire. The inaugural 200-piece exhibition runs through October, after which the venue closes for several months of renovations.

title: Working while going through IVF: 'I didn't want my employers to know about it because of my career'
subhed: Women across France undergoing assisted reproductive treatment told Le Monde the challenges they face in balancing their professional lives with medical appointments.  These women shared the 'emotional and physical'  exhaustion they have tried to hide and the discrimination they have experienced.

In a small French village, a father is charged with sexual abuse of 34 victims aged 3 to 9
subhed: A tree, a rope, a letter. After surviving a suicide attempt, a man revealed he had committed numerous crimes against children. After a year-long and controversial investigation, he was charged, on April 10, with rape and sexual assault of minors and recording child pornography.

title: The Lebrun brothers and the French team's dream of a first world table tennis title is 'in the back of their minds'
subhed: With five players ranked in the world Top 30, the French national table tennis team is once again a strong contender for a medal at the World Team Championships, the final stage of which is taking place from May 2 to May 10 in London.

title: Senegal's lawyers face a dilemma as new anti-LGBTQ+ law takes effect
subhed: Since the bill was enacted on March 30, authorities have arrested at least 100 people across the country for 'acts against nature.' Many lawyers have chosen to distance themselves from Senegalese defendants, fearing potential reprisals against themselves or their loved ones.
